speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, my lord.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Who knocks?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: One from the Lord Stanley.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: What is ’t o’clock?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Upon the stroke of four.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Cannot my Lord Stanley sleep these tedious nights?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: So it appears by that I have to say.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: First, he commends him to your noble self.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: What then?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Then certifies your Lordship that this night

speaker: MESSENGER
text: He dreamt the boar had razèd off his helm.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Besides, he says there are two councils kept,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And that may be determined at the one

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Which may make you and him to rue at th’ other.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Therefore he sends to know your Lordship’s pleasure,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: If you will presently take horse with him

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And with all speed post with him toward the north

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To shun the danger that his soul divines.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Go, fellow, go. Return unto thy lord.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Bid him not fear the separated council.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: His Honor and myself are at the one,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And at the other is my good friend Catesby,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Where nothing can proceed that toucheth us

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Whereof I shall not have intelligence.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Tell him his fears are shallow, without instance.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And for his dreams, I wonder he’s so simple

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To trust the mock’ry of unquiet slumbers.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To fly the boar before the boar pursues

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Were to incense the boar to follow us

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And make pursuit where he did mean no chase.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Go, bid thy master rise and come to me,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And we will both together to the Tower,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Where he shall see the boar will use us kindly.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I’ll go, my lord, and tell him what you say.

speaker: CATESBY
text: Many good morrows to my noble lord.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Good morrow, Catesby. You are early stirring.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: What news, what news in this our tott’ring state?

speaker: CATESBY
text: It is a reeling world indeed, my lord,

speaker: CATESBY
text: And I believe will never stand upright

speaker: CATESBY
text: Till Richard wear the garland of the realm.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: How “wear the garland”? Dost thou mean the crown?

speaker: CATESBY
text: Ay, my good lord.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I’ll have this crown of mine cut from my shoulders

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Before I’ll see the crown so foul misplaced.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But canst thou guess that he doth aim at it?

speaker: CATESBY
text: Ay, on my life, and hopes to find you forward

speaker: CATESBY
text: Upon his party for the gain thereof;

speaker: CATESBY
text: And thereupon he sends you this good news,

speaker: CATESBY
text: That this same very day your enemies,

speaker: CATESBY
text: The kindred of the Queen, must die at Pomfret.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Indeed, I am no mourner for that news,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Because they have been still my adversaries.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But that I’ll give my voice on Richard’s side

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To bar my master’s heirs in true descent,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: God knows I will not do it, to the death.

speaker: CATESBY
text: God keep your Lordship in that gracious mind.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But I shall laugh at this a twelve-month hence,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: That they which brought me in my master’s hate,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I live to look upon their tragedy.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Well, Catesby, ere a fortnight make me older

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I’ll send some packing that yet think not on ’t.

speaker: CATESBY
text: ’Tis a vile thing to die, my gracious lord,

speaker: CATESBY
text: When men are unprepared and look not for it.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: O monstrous, monstrous! And so falls it out

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With Rivers, Vaughan, Grey; and so ’twill do

speaker: HASTINGS
text: With some men else that think themselves as safe

speaker: HASTINGS
text: As thou and I, who, as thou know’st, are dear

speaker: HASTINGS
text: To princely Richard and to Buckingham.

speaker: CATESBY
text: The Princes both make high account of you—

speaker: CATESBY
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              For they account his head upon the Bridge.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I know they do, and I have well deserved it.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Come on, come on. Where is your boar-spear, man?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Fear you the boar and go so unprovided?

speaker: STANLEY
text: My lord, good morrow.—Good morrow, Catesby.—

speaker: STANLEY
text: You may jest on, but, by the Holy Rood,

speaker: STANLEY
text: I do not like these several councils, I.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: My lord, I hold my life as dear as you do yours,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And never in my days, I do protest,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Was it so precious to me as ’tis now.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Think you but that I know our state secure,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I would be so triumphant as I am?

speaker: STANLEY
text: The lords at Pomfret, when they rode from London,

speaker: STANLEY
text: Were jocund and supposed their states were sure,

speaker: STANLEY
text: And they indeed had no cause to mistrust;

speaker: STANLEY
text: But yet you see how soon the day o’ercast.

speaker: STANLEY
text: This sudden stab of rancor I misdoubt.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Pray God, I say, I prove a needless coward!

speaker: STANLEY
text: What, shall we toward the Tower? The day is spent.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Come, come. Have with you. Wot you what, my lord?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Today the lords you talked of are beheaded.

speaker: STANLEY
text: They, for their truth, might better wear their heads

speaker: STANLEY
text: Than some that have accused them wear their hats.

speaker: STANLEY
text: But come, my lord, let’s away.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Go on before. I’ll talk with this good fellow.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: How now, sirrah? How goes the world with thee?

speaker: PURSUIVANT
text: The better that your Lordship please to ask.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I tell thee, man, ’tis better with me now

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Than when thou met’st me last where now we meet.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Then was I going prisoner to the Tower

speaker: HASTINGS
text: By the suggestion of the Queen’s allies.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: But now, I tell thee—keep it to thyself—

speaker: HASTINGS
text: This day those enemies are put to death,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: And I in better state than e’er I was.

speaker: PURSUIVANT
text: God hold it, to your Honor’s good content!

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Gramercy, fellow. There, drink that for me.

speaker: PURSUIVANT
text: I thank your Honor.

speaker: PRIEST
text: Well met, my lord. I am glad to see your Honor.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I thank thee, good Sir John, with all my heart.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I am in your debt for your last exercise.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Come the next sabbath, and I will content you.

speaker: PRIEST
text: I’ll wait upon your Lordship.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: What, talking with a priest, Lord Chamberlain?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your friends at Pomfret, they do need the priest;

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Your Honor hath no shriving work in hand.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Good faith, and when I met this holy man,

speaker: HASTINGS
text: The men you talk of came into my mind.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: What, go you toward the Tower?

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I do, my lord, but long I cannot stay there.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: I shall return before your Lordship thence.

speaker: HASTINGS
text: Nay, like enough, for I stay dinner there.

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: And supper too, although thou know’st it not.—

speaker: BUCKINGHAM
text: Come, will you go?

speaker: HASTINGS
text: I’ll wait upon your Lordship.
